- E2E: CLI explain output formatting

 {"id":"bd-3hxdl","title":"Task: Add last_output_ts field to pane status","description":"## What

Add `last_output_ts` field tracking when the pane last produced output.

## Why

Timeout-based stuck detection needs to know when output last changed.
"No output for 5 minutes" is a strong signal the agent is stuck.
Currently this requires external state tracking.

## Implementation

```go
type PaneOutputTracker struct {
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    lastOutput  map[int]time.Time  // pane_index -> last output time
}

func (t *PaneOutputTracker) Update(paneIndex int) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastOutput[paneIndex] = time.Now()
}

func (t *PaneOutputTracker) Get(paneIndex int) time.Time {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    return t.lastOutput[paneIndex]
}
```

## State Persistence

- Option A: In-memory only (reset on ntm restart) - simpler
- Option B: Persist to session metadata file - survives restarts

Recommend Option A for MVP, add persistence later if needed.

## How to Detect Output Changes

During status gathering, compare current pane content hash with previous.
If changed, update timestamp.

```go
func checkOutputChanged(pane int) bool {
    current := capturePaneContent(pane)
    hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(current))
    if hash != lastHash[pane] {
        lastHash[pane] = hash
        return true
    }
    return false
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T23:15:00Z",  // ISO 8601
    "seconds_since_output": 312                 // Convenience field
  }]
}
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Mock time, verify timestamp updates
- Integration test: Verify with actual pane output changes","notes":"Feature already implemented in status_enrich.go. getLastOutput function tracks pane output timestamps and enrichAgentStatus populates LastOutputTS and SecondsSinceOutput fields correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:23.314896135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:38:40.617201274Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:38:40.617114601Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hxdl","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3i16o","title":"Task: Add ensemble config section to ~/.config/ntm/config.toml","description":"# Task: Add ensemble config section to config.toml

Define ensemble defaults in the main config file for users who prefer TOML over YAML.

## Config Shape (proposal)
```toml
[ensemble]
# Defaults used when flags not provided
default_ensemble = "architecture-review"
agent_mix = "cc=3,cod=2,gmi=1"
assignment = "affinity"
# Tier defaults
mode_tier_default = "core"     # core|advanced|experimental
allow_advanced = false         # explicit opt-in

[ensemble.synthesis]
strategy = "deliberative"
synthesizer_mode = "meta-cognitive"

[ensemble.cache]
enabled = true
ttl_minutes = 60
cache_dir = "~/.cache/ntm/context-packs"

[ensemble.budget]
per_agent = 5000
total = 30000
synthesis = 8000
context_pack = 2000

[ensemble.early_stop]
enabled = true
min_agents = 3
findings_threshold = 0.15
similarity_threshold = 0.7
window_size = 3
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config loads with sensible defaults
- CLI flags override config values
- Validation errors are clear and actionable
- Documentation updated with examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistySpring","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:19:15.271736208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:17:44.686328790Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:17:44.686158800Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i16o","depends_on_id":"bd-1kthb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3i16o","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3iglm","title":"Add ntm assign --retry for retrying failed assignments","description":"## Overview
Retry failed assignments with a single command.

## Use Cases
1. Agent crashed mid-task, want to retry on same or different agent
2. Agent gave up due to transient issue
3. Batch retry all failures after investigation

## Usage
```bash
# Retry specific bead
ntm assign myproject --retry=bd-xyz

# Retry specific bead to specific agent
ntm assign myproject --retry=bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Retry all failed assignments
ntm assign myproject --retry-failed

# Retry all failed for a specific agent type
ntm assign myproject --retry-failed --agent=codex

# Retry with different strategy (reassign to best agent)
ntm assign myproject --retry-failed --strategy=quality
```

## How It Works
1. Find failed assignments matching filter
2. Clear the failed assignment (reset status)
3. If --to-pane: Assign to specific pane
4. Else: Use strategy to pick best agent
5. Create new assignment, send prompt
6. Return results

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "retry",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "retried": [
      {
        "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
        "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
        "pane": 4,
        "agent_type": "claude",
        "agent_name": "RedStone",
        "status": "assigned",
        "prompt_sent": true,
        "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
        "previous_pane": 3,
        "previous_agent": "BlueLake",
        "previous_fail_reason": "agent crashed",
        "retry_count": 1
      }
    ],
    "skipped": [
      {
        "bead_id": "bd-abc",
        "reason": "no idle agents available"
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "total_failed": 5,
      "retried_count": 4,
      "skipped_count": 1
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Retry vs Reassign
- **--retry**: For failed assignments only
- **--reassign**: For any assignment (working, assigned, failed)

## Error Handling
- No failed assignments: Return success with empty retried array
- No idle agents: Add to skipped with reason
- Bead no longer exists: Add to skipped with reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Retry single bead by ID
- [ ] Retry to specific pane
- [ ] Retry all failed (--retry-failed)
- [ ] Filter by agent type
- [ ] Uses strategy for new assignment
- [ ] Preserves assignment history (previous_* fields)
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Includes retry_count

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Retry logic
- Unit test: Filter matching
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Retry workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:54:48.388293630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:40:02.793664994Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:40:02.793619128Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iglm","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3iglm","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ihtb","title":"Unit Tests: PromptInjector timing and formatting","description":"## Purpose
Unit tests for PromptInjector covering prompt formatting, staggered delivery, and agent-specific quirks.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/prompt_injector_test.go
package swarm

func TestPromptInjector_FormatPrompt(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        agentType string
        prompt    string
        expected  string
    }{
        {
            name:      "codex_double_enter",
            agentType: "cod",
            prompt:    "Run the tests",
            expected:  "\n\nRun the tests\n",
        },
        {
            name:      "claude_normal",
            agentType: "cc",
            prompt:    "Read AGENTS.md",
            expected:  "Read AGENTS.md\n",
        },
        {
            name:      "gemini_normal",
            agentType: "gmi",
            prompt:    "Fix the bug",
            expected:  "Fix the bug\n",
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            injector := NewPromptInjector(nil, nil)
            result := injector.formatPrompt(tt.agentType, tt.prompt)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Agent: %s, Input: %q", tt.agentType, tt.prompt)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Output: %q", result)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
        })
    }
}

func TestPromptInjector_StaggeredDelivery(t *testing.T) {
    mockPaneSpawner := &MockPaneSpawner{}
    tracker := ratelimit.NewRateLimitTracker("")
    
    injector := NewPromptInjector(mockPaneSpawner, tracker)
    injector.baseDelay = 100 * time.Millisecond
    
    panes := []string{"session:1.0", "session:1.1", "session:1.2"}
    prompt := "Start work"
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Starting staggered delivery to 3 panes")
    
    start := time.Now()
    var deliveryTimes []time.Duration
    
    for _, pane := range panes {
        t.Logf("[TEST] Injecting to %s", pane)
        _, err := injector.InjectPrompt(pane, "cc", prompt)
        require.NoError(t, err)
        deliveryTimes = append(deliveryTimes, time.Since(start))
    }
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Delivery times: %v", deliveryTimes)
    
    // Verify staggering
    for i := 1; i < len(deliveryTimes); i++ {
        gap := deliveryTimes[i] - deliveryTimes[i-1]
        t.Logf("[TEST] Gap %d->%d: %v", i-1, i, gap)
        assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, gap, 90*time.Millisecond)
    }
}

func TestPromptInjector_ReviewModePrompts(t *testing.T) {
    injector := NewPromptInjector(nil, nil)
    
    prompt := injector.getReviewModePrompt("gmi")
    t.Logf("[TEST] Review mode prompt for gmi: %s", prompt[:50])
    
    assert.Contains(t, prompt, "review")
    assert.Contains(t, prompt, "code quality")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test prompt formatting for all agent types
- [ ] Test Codex double-Enter quirk handling
- [ ] Test staggered delivery timing
- [ ] Test rate limit integration
- [ ] Test review-mode prompts
- [ ] Detailed test logging with [TEST] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:32:50.813292584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:12:06.533845924Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:12:06.533768718Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ihtb","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
